title: Orchis athensis

Orchis mascula, also known as the early purple orchid, is a type of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae, native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of health issues, including digestive problems, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Orchis mascula include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties.
